title: Onboarding — CLIENT complete flow: 3 slides, Back hidden on slide 1, Get Started on last slide, navigate to home, re-open skips onboarding

preconditions.description: Expo web app is running and reachable at the configured base URL.
A verified CLIENT account exists and credentials are available (DATA-1, DATA-2).
localStorage does NOT contain 'companion_onboarding_complete' (or is cleared before this test). The /(client)/_layout.tsx guard must redirect to onboarding.

journey.description: Open login screen with a fresh browser context (no localStorage).
After login, layout guard detects no onboarding completion and redirects to onboarding screen.
Next button is visible on slide 1.
Navigate back to slide 1 using Back button.
Confirm we are back on slide 1.
Tap Get Started on the last slide to complete onboarding.
markOnboardingComplete() is called, then router.replace to /(client)/home.
Reload the page to simulate re-opening the app and test onboarding skip.
After reload, session is restored and onboarding is marked complete — layout guard skips onboarding.

assertions.description: Fresh CLIENT user is redirected to onboarding.
Back button is NOT visible on slide 1 (currentSlide === 0 hides Back).

target.text: Your trusted companion experience starts here.

assertions.description: Back button IS visible on slide 2 (currentSlide > 0).
Button label changes to 'Get Started' on the last slide.
Next button is no longer present on the last slide.
After Get Started, navigates to CLIENT home.
After reload, layout guard reads onboarding complete from localStorage and routes directly to home — onboarding is skipped.

manualValidationRequired.reason: No supported native automation runner is currently configured.
No supported native automation runner is currently configured.

notes: This test requires a fresh browser context with no localStorage (use Playwright's browser.newContext() or page.evaluate to clear localStorage before step 1).
The 'companion_onboarding_complete' key in AsyncStorage on web maps to localStorage. The exact key name should be verified against onboarding-storage.ts.
Slide progress indicator text (e.g., '1 / 3') is visible in the UI but not asserted to keep test robust against slide count changes.
The Back button renders conditionally: {currentSlide > 0 && <Pressable>Back</Pressable>}. On slide 1 (index 0) it is not in the DOM at all.
The slide 3 CLIENT body text is 'Your first session is just a few taps away.' — not asserted here but useful for debugging.
